title: Roasted Australian Pork Belly Skewers
description: A malty, crispy, monsoon Australian dish featuring pork belly slices, binchotan-charcoal, lacto-vegetarian-friendly, this recipe is perfect for a dinner party
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together malt syrup, brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sliced pork belly to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the malt syrup mixture.
Thread the coated pork belly slices onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast the skewers in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the pork is crispy on the outside and cooked through.
While the skewers are roasting, prepare the binchotan charcoal for smoking by heating it over medium heat.
Once the skewers are done roasting, place them on the binchotan charcoal and smoke for 5 minutes, or until the skewers are fragrant with smoke.
Remove the skewers from the heat and brush with lemon juice.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
